name: New Hampshire Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to New Hampshire - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Licensed Dietitians
provider.parentOrganization: New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC)

address.description:
Office of Professional Licensure and Certification
7 Eagle Square
Concord, NH 03301

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: A letter verifying RD status sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration to the Board.

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Required if RD card was obtained on or after 1/1/2001. Must be sent from the degree-granting institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Verification of Internship Hours
requiredDocuments.description: Required if RD card was obtained on or after 1/1/2001. Sent directly from the internship supervisor.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a New Hampshire dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial license application fee is $110.

mainEntity.name: When does the New Hampshire license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: New Hampshire dietitian licenses must be renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $110. A $150 reinstatement fee applies to late submissions.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for New Hampshire?

acceptedAnswer.text: Licensees must complete 20 hours of approved continuing education within the preceding two years to qualify for license renewal.

mainEntity.name: Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: New Hampshire has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to New Hampshire?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
